'- Some users felt that the expiration time of a request was a little bit long. - Most of the users were confused by the document status showing as “Accepted”, leading them to believe that the document had already been shared, even though they hadn’t confirmed sharing yet - Some users thought that personal information was editable due to its design. - Some users encountered difficulty clicking the “Reject”button due to its small size. - A few users noted that the “Reject” button's green color seemed irrelevant to its intended action. - Almost all users found the sharing animation screen appealing, however, a few thought this page meant that documents had already been shared. -The status “Not currently in your digital documents” was unclear to some users, leading to confusion about the meaning of “digital documents”. - Some users felt that the “Request” button was too small. - None of the users noticed the button “Got it” on the notification message. - A few users found the message unclear, thinking they needed to manually sign the requested document before uploading it “Pre-signed version” - Some users experienced confusion on specific screens, especially in the InsuranceCoscenarios, where the success page description indicated successfully sharing documents with BankCoinstead of InsuranceCo. - Most of the user understanding was not aligned with the current process and how the documents can be used on UAE PASS. - Some users encountered difficulty clicking the “Cancel”button due to its small size. - A few users noted that the “Cancel” button's green color seemed irrelevant to its intended action. - Some users wondered why the optional document was pre-checked; they preferred to keep the optional documents unselected unless they need them.'

U-Ask, a unified AI-powered chatbot for the UAE’s government services, It utilises generative AI to answer queries regarding the UAE government’s services for citizens, residents, workers, students, visitors and investors.

The Dubai.AI platform is a comprehensive and interactive source of information about the city, continuously populated with data from official sources. It acts as a personal digital assistant for users, designed using generative AI to answer their queries in real-time.

Uploaded: - Almost all users did not notice the filter inside the screen as it was too small - A few users found that the icon next to each document in the list was not really helping in recognizing the type. - Some users said that the document type and name are supposed to be automatically added. - Most of the users didn’t understand the action “Store to documents” - There was no enough space between “Edit Selection” button and the arrow, and some users thought they have different actions and wanted to have enough space to be more comfortable on which component they want to click.

User Behavior Analytics: The app could analyze user behavior to understand the user's pain points and help identify areas that can improve their experience.
Mobile app analytics platform designed to understand user behavior and optimize app functionality.

An app measurement solution that provides insights on app usage and user engagement. It’s available at no charge and integrates across Firebase features.   Key features:   Audience Segmentation: Custom audiences can be defined based on device data, custom events, or user properties. These audiences can be used with other Firebase features when targeting new features or notification messages.User Insights: Surfaces data about user behavior in your iOS and Android apps, enabling you to make better decisions about your product and marketing optimization.Unlimited Reporting: Provides unlimited reporting on up to 500 distinct events.Realtime Analytics: Understand user behavior and view live usage data with realtime reporting.

Key features:   Experience Analytics: Provides tools like session replay, heatmaps, and user journeys to help you deliver the perfect app experience.Product Analytics: Provides reports automatically, allowing understand user segments, see where users drop off and analyze retention & churn.Secure & Private: It prioritizes keeping your data secure.

User Behavior Analytics: The app could analyze user behavior to understand the user's pain points and help identify areas that can improve their experience. Contextual Search: A search feature that not only searches for file names but also the content within the documents. Temporary Access: Users could grant temporary access to certain documents for a specified period. Automatic document resharing upon renewal
Idea is not feasible as we are not storing the data on the UAE PASS application

Multi-Language Support through AI/ Multi-Language Support: To cater to the diverse population of the UAE, the app could offer support for multiple languages. Selective Sharing: Allow users to select specific parts of a document to share, keeping the rest private. Comments on sharing requests: Allow service providers to annotate or comment on shared documents, facilitating better communication.

Gamification: To encourage regular use and proper security practices, gamification elements could be introduced. File Organization: Users could have the ability to create folders and tags to organize their documents. Revocable Access: Users should be able to revoke access to shared documents at any time. Monetization: Place for service providers to host services offered by UAE PASS and introduce a usage Fees/ Subscription Model Most used documents: Wallet: Allow documents to be added to the mobile wallet

column3: We hope to onboard few issuers for which the count of documents retrieved for users is very high. For e.g. we hope to onboard FTA. A user may have Tax residency documents from many past years (for e.g. since 1995 which results in count of documents to be above 30-40). In such cases a user will see a prefetch screen with too many listings. Even choosing a specific document will be very tough for the user.

Value delivered to users by December
High - enhances a main flow in the application

Sharing prioritized over adding documents: the number of documents issued per unique DV user each month has consistently been between 0.9-1, while the number of successful sharing requests per unique DV user has been significantly lower (between 0.5-0.6 in the last half-year). This is partially driven by a low successful sharing rate (59% in June, with 29% of sharing requests having no action rather than failing).
In the unsuccessful sharing deep-dive, we saw that 17% of 'No action' sharing request failures had the documents not available
